master_row.title: Field Coherence Metric
master_row.canonical_statement: This metric measures the degree of coherence between two fields on the Trawinistic manifold.
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as an expectation / trace over state relating φ.
master_row.dictionary: Given two field configurations (_1) and (_2) on (M), the field coherence metric (C(_1,_2)) is defined as (C = _1,_2 _1 , _2 ), where (,) denotes the Trawinistic inner product and ( =,).
master_row.encyclopedia: The field coherence metric measures normalized alignment between two field states. Values near $1$ indicate strong coherence; values near $0$ indicate decoherence or orthogonality. Interpretive role: This metric gives the registry a clean scalar handle on alignment, decoherence, and modal overlap. It is especially useful once curvature and singular corrections begin to influence how fields interact near the anchor.
